description_long: This is a wooden baseball bat with a natural, light tan finish, featuring a cylindrical handle that tapers into a thicker barrel. The barrel is wrapped with several dark, metallic nails or spikes protruding outward, giving it a rugged and menacing appearance. The wood surface shows signs of wear, with some discoloration and minor scuffs, suggesting frequent use or intentional distressing. The handle is smooth and lacks any grip tape, maintaining the same wood texture throughout. The end cap is rounded and integrated into the wood, with no distinct separation or additional material. There are no visible brand logos, manufacturer names, or decorative markings on the bat.
description: A worn, light tan wooden baseball bat with protruding metal spikes around the barrel and a smooth, unwrapped handle.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the bat, with metal spikes clearly visible on the barrel and a smooth, uninterrupted handle.
description_view_1: Front-on view down the handle, emphasizing the circular end cap and the clustered arrangement of spikes at the far end.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, again displaying the full length and the distribution of spikes, with the wood grain and wear visible.
description_view_3: Barrel-end view, highlighting the dense grouping of spikes protruding from the barrel and the round end cap at the handle.
